summary: Cleaned up Dolphin DVD decomp code while adding/moving DVD API declarations and implementing several DVD command helpers needed for matching. The PR deleted dvd_private.h after review feedback that static function prototypes should not live in headers, moved those forward declarations into dvd.c, exposed DVDLowCallback/DVDLowClearCallback in the public DVD header, renamed the internal disk-ID pointer from IDShouldBe to currID, made autoInvalidation static near other globals, and added synchronous/asynchronous stream, seek, inquiry, and cancel-all helpers.
what_changed: Deleted src/dolphin/dvd/dvd_private.h, which had contained only static function prototypes for dvd.c-local callbacks and state functions.
Moved the dvd_private.h static prototypes directly into src/dolphin/dvd/dvd.c under a comment identifying them as callback/state-change declarations.
Added DVDLowCallback typedef and DVDLowClearCallback declaration to include/dolphin/dvd/dvd.h, replacing their prior local declaration in dvd.c.
Renamed static DVDDiskID* IDShouldBe to currID and updated uses in DVDInit, stateCheckID, and stateCheckID3.
Added static volatile BOOL CancelAllSyncComplete for synchronous cancel-all completion tracking.
Moved BOOL autoInvalidation = TRUE into the static global area as static BOOL autoInvalidation = TRUE, reducing exported/global visibility.

Added DVDSeekAbsAsyncPrio, DVDPrepareStreamAbsAsync, DVDCancelStreamAsync, DVDCancelStream, DVDStopStreamAtEndAsync, DVDInquiryAsync, DVDCancelAll, cbForCancelStreamSync, and cbForCancelAllSync implementations in dvd.c.
Changed DVDGetDriveStatus() to DVDGetDriveStatus(void), matching explicit no-argument C prototype style.
Expanded DVDGetCurrentDiskID from a one-line function into a normal multi-line definition without changing its apparent behavior.
smart_moves: Responded to header hygiene feedback by removing a private header that only declared static functions; this keeps internal linkage declarations in the translation unit that owns them.
Publicly declared DVDLowCallback and DVDLowClearCallback in include/dolphin/dvd/dvd.h because they are not dvd.c-local static internals and are needed as shared DVD interface types/functions.

Kept synchronous wrappers such as DVDCancelStream and DVDCancelAll thin around their async forms, using OSDisableInterrupts/OSRestoreInterrupts, OSSleepThread, and OSWakeupThread on __DVDThreadQueue to mirror typical Dolphin SDK blocking-call structure.
Used command numeric assignments in small API wrappers before calling issueCommand, preserving the established pattern already visible in DVDReadAbsAsyncPrio and DVDReadDiskID.
Reduced symbol leakage by making autoInvalidation static after moving it near other file-scope state.
decomp_lessons: Do not put prototypes for static C functions in a header unless they are inline definitions; use file-local forward declarations in the C file instead.
When a callback typedef or function is part of cross-file API surface, move it to the public include header rather than declaring it ad hoc inside a C file.
For matching-oriented cleanup, variable naming can matter: the PR changed IDShouldBe to currID to better describe the current disk ID pointer used during ID checks.
Keep internal state globals grouped near related DVD state variables; this makes linkage and ordering clearer and may help matching/data layout review.
Synchronous DVD APIs in this codebase are commonly implemented as async command submission plus an interrupt-disabled sleep/wakeup loop on __DVDThreadQueue.
naming_conventions: DVD API functions use the DVD-prefixed SDK-style names, e.g. DVDSeekAbsAsyncPrio, DVDPrepareStreamAbsAsync, DVDCancelStreamAsync, DVDCancelStream, DVDStopStreamAtEndAsync, DVDInquiryAsync, DVDCancelAll.
DVD low-level callback type is named DVDLowCallback and takes u32 intType.
Private callback helpers use cbFor... names, e.g. cbForCancelAllSync, cbForCancelStreamSync, cbForStateBusy, cbForStateCheckID3.
DVD state helpers use state... names, e.g. stateBusy, stateCheckID2, stateCoverClosed_CMD, stateGoToRetry.
No-argument functions should use an explicit void parameter list, as shown by DVDGetDriveStatus(void).
Internal current disk ID pointer was renamed currID rather than IDShouldBe.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use file-local static forward declarations to control function ordering without leaking static symbols through headers.
Add thin wrapper functions with local BOOL idle/result variables and command field assignments before issueCommand, matching existing DVD wrapper structure.
For blocking wrappers, store async callback results into command block fields or completion flags, then wake __DVDThreadQueue from the callback.
Make globals static when they are translation-unit local; this may affect symbol visibility and better match intended SDK/internal linkage.
Use volatile access for command block state polling in DVDCancelStream: ((volatile DVDCommandBlock*) block)->state.
review_feedback: ribbanya reviewed src/dolphin/dvd/dvd_private.h and said: "static methods should not appear in header files, unless they are inlines. Delete this file, please. Use forward declarations in the C file where necessary."
The final diff reflects that feedback by deleting dvd_private.h and moving static prototypes into dvd.c.
follow_up_queries: Check later DVD PRs for whether the newly added DVD stream/inquiry/cancel functions were further matched or prototype-corrected.
Search for remaining private headers that only contain static function declarations and apply the same cleanup pattern.

Verify whether public declarations for DVDSeekAbsAsyncPrio, DVDPrepareStreamAbsAsync, DVDCancelStreamAsync, DVDCancelStream, DVDStopStreamAtEndAsync, DVDInquiryAsync, and DVDCancelAll were added in a later header PR if needed.
Look for additional Dolphin SDK naming evidence around currID versus IDShouldBe and autoInvalidation linkage.
